rulings.Case Title: LOUIS MARCELLI VS. MOVIE MOVERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re Ntc Of Pltf Louis Marcelli'S Motiopn To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 18, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF LOUIS MARCELLI Re Ntc Of Pltf Louis Marcelli'S Motiopn To Tax Costs The motion to tax costs sought by Defendant "The Malpaso Company, Inc." is granted in part, and denied in part. Malpaso Company Inc. was the prevailing party in the summary judgment motion. The motion to tax costs is granted as to $853.52 in messenger costs to file documents with the Court. This is not service of process pursuant to CCP § 1033.5(a)(4), and the Court declines to exercise its discretion to award these particular costs under CCP § 1033.5(c)(4) based on the showing before me. The motion to tax costs is granted as to the costs of the deposition of plaintiff. There is insufficient proof that Defendant noticed this deposition or incurred any cost for it; Exhibit K to the Benbow declaration is an invoice for $38.00. As to the other deposition costs, Defendant has failed to meet its evidentiary burden to show that this Defendant issued these deposition subpoenas, and what the items on these invoices (Exhibit L) are intended to cover. The motion to tax costs is thus granted as to all of the "deposition" charges in the amount of $2069.82. The total amount of costs taxed (i.e., deducted) is $2905.34 (If there is a hearing on this motion, moving party is requested to bring a copy of the Defendant's Memorandum of Costs filed in this case. It was not provided with the moving papers.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MATS NAHLINDER VS. MARKMONITOR, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 18, 2013, Line 33, Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Denied. At the hearing on November 26, 2012, Pro Tem Judge Watson indicated he would grant Defendant's motion to compel requiring Plaintiff to disclose the name of the party with whom plaintiff was contracting. Pro Tem Judge Watson has already ruled that the information sought is relevant, and the motion to quash is denied on that ground. As to the question of timeliness, the motion to quash is also denied. Defendant filed its motion to compel in a timely way, leaving enough time to obtain a ruling from the Court on November 26, 2012, in advance of the November 30, 2012 subpoena deadline. Counsel for plaintiff left the courtroom on November 26, 2012 before defendant's counsel was able to obtain a signed order or confer with plaintiff's counsel. That afternoon, defense counsel submitted a proposed form of order to plaintiff's counsel. Plaintiff neither responded to the proposed order nor disclosed the name of the party with whom Plaintiff was contracting, despite the Pro Tem Judge's indication at the hearing on November 26, 2012 that he would grant Defendant's motion. Defendant acted with diligence in seeking to obtain the name of the contracting party from Plaintiff, but by the time Defendant could obtain the Court's signature on the Order, it was December 3, 2012, and it then took until December 7, 2012 to obtain a copy of the order and file a notice of entry of order. Plaintiff's counsel did not disclose the name until December 12, 2012, after the subpoena deadline had passed. Under all of the circumstances, the motion to quash the subpoena as untimely is denied. Sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. ==(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TOSHIA HOLOWAY VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY, A PUBLIC AGENCY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Conduct A Mental Exam Of Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 18, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 2, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY, A PUBLIC AGENCY's Motion To Conduct A Mental Exam Of Plaintiff. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S Motion to Conduct a Medical Examination pursuant to C.C.P. § 2032.310 is GRANTED for good cause shown. Order to issue at hearing on the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. KE Y ZHAO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 18, 2013, Line 20, PLAINTIFF PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings Motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend. In the answer, defendant "admits that all of the statements of the Complaint are correct." No opposition to the motion was filed. Plaintiff shall file a memorandum of costs and seek to enter judgment in accordance with the Code of Civil Procedure; the Court will not award costs as part of this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROTH RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, INC. VS. IVONNE CORTEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Response To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 18, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 5, PLAINTIFF ROTH RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, INC.'s Motion For Order Compelling Response To Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Defendant IVONNE CORTEZ shall serve responses, without objection, to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories (Set One), Requests for Admission (Set One), Request for Production of Documents (Set One), and Special Interrogatories (Set One) on or before March 1, 2013. Defendant's request for sanctions in the amount of $775.00 is GRANTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
